## Summary

Makes every manuscript title in the dashboard evaluation history table a clickable hyperlink, and renames the confusing "Below standard" status badge to "Completed."

**Problems fixed:**

1. **No hyperlinks on manuscript titles** — Authors couldn't click a manuscript name to go to its report or evaluation page. Now every title is a link: completed evals go to `/reports/{jobId}`, running/failed evals go to `/evaluate/{jobId}`.

2. **"Below standard" label was confusing** — When scores are `—` (null), "Below standard" implies the manuscript was judged and found wanting, when actually the evaluation may have completed without scoring or the scores simply aren't displayed. Renamed to "Completed" — the score bars and numbers already communicate threshold position. The status column should communicate whether the evaluation finished, not re-judge the score.

3. **Badge styling** — Added a proper `.rg-status--completed` CSS class (neutral warm tone) instead of falling through to the amber `improving` style.

**Key changes:**
- `EvaluationHistoryTable.jsx`: Title cell is now an `<a>` tag linking to `row.reportHref`
- `globals.css`: Added `a.rg-history-title-link` styles + `.rg-status--completed` badge
- `getDashboardEvaluations.ts`: Added comment clarifying null-score handling

## Scope

**Touches:** Dashboard evaluation history table, CSS, dashboard status derivation
**Does NOT touch:** Pipeline, evaluation logic, report pages, ledger components

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

Minimal risk — UI-only changes to the dashboard table. The `reportHref` field already existed on every row; we just wrapped the title in an `<a>` tag. Status label change from "Below standard" → "Completed" is cosmetic only; the underlying `below_standard` status value is unchanged in the data layer.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — no extraction or pipeline code modified
